description: Local structured evidence/RAG catalog for fictional, unclassified training and exercise planning. The catalog uses qualitative pattern cards and does not claim precise statistical failure rates.

pattern_cards.description: Assumptions that external partners, hosts, civil authorities, or supporting organizations remain aligned and available.
risk_factors: partner priorities can shift under pressure
support may be delayed by approval or capacity limits
coordination paths can become unclear when conditions change
mitigation_patterns: name a liaison or coordination owner
define a reduced-scope branch if support is delayed
pattern_cards.safe_historical_analog_summary: Coalition plans often face political availability shifts.
pattern_cards.doctrine_note: Assumptions require validation, and plans should include branches or sequels when partner support changes.

pattern_cards.description: Assumptions that movement, sustainment, maintenance, staging, and distribution remain continuous enough for the plan.
risk_factors: movement capacity may be overestimated
staging or distribution nodes may become constrained
maintenance and replenishment timing may lag demand
pattern_cards.safe_historical_analog_summary: Logistics assumptions can fail when routes, timing, or infrastructure are unverified.
pattern_cards.doctrine_note: Operating environments change, so logistics assumptions require active monitoring and branch options.

pattern_cards.description: Assumptions that communications paths, reports, and shared updates remain timely, accurate, and usable.
risk_factors: communication paths may degrade or become congested
shared understanding can lag actual conditions
missed reports can hide assumption failure
mitigation_patterns: tie missed reports to validation actions
pattern_cards.safe_historical_analog_summary: Communications assumptions are fragile under degraded or contested environments.
pattern_cards.doctrine_note: Commanders need updated information requirements, and uncertainty must be actively monitored.

pattern_cards.description: Assumptions that estimates, forecasts, status reports, and pattern recognition remain reliable enough for decisions.
risk_factors: information may be stale or incomplete
initial estimates may not reflect changing conditions
decision patterns can be biased by familiar but wrong cues
observable_indicators: new anomaly against the current estimate
mitigation_patterns: run challenge reviews on key estimates
pattern_cards.safe_historical_analog_summary: Plans can degrade when early estimates are treated as fixed facts.
pattern_cards.doctrine_note: Assumptions require validation, and commanders need updated information requirements as conditions change.

pattern_cards.description: Assumptions that civilian movement, compliance, demand, or public response remains predictable.
risk_factors: civilian behavior may change quickly under stress
public demand can exceed expected coordination capacity
rumors or uncertainty can alter movement patterns
pattern_cards.safe_historical_analog_summary: Civil behavior assumptions can shift when information, access, or perceived risk changes.
pattern_cards.doctrine_note: Operating environments change, and uncertainty about civilian behavior must be actively monitored.

pattern_cards.description: Assumptions that ports, routes, power, facilities, bridges, or other infrastructure remain usable.
risk_factors: single infrastructure nodes can constrain the whole plan
facility status may be unverified
infrastructure capacity can degrade under load
mitigation_patterns: stage only critical tasks through constrained nodes
pattern_cards.safe_historical_analog_summary: Infrastructure assumptions often fail when capacity, access, or status is not validated.
pattern_cards.doctrine_note: Plans should include branches or sequels when infrastructure availability changes.

pattern_cards.description: Assumptions that weather, terrain, sea state, visibility, or environmental limits remain acceptable.
risk_factors: environmental conditions can change faster than planning cycles
movement or visibility may degrade
condition thresholds may be undefined
mitigation_patterns: reduce exposed tasks when thresholds are crossed
pattern_cards.safe_historical_analog_summary: Weather and environmental assumptions can degrade timing, movement, and observation.
pattern_cards.doctrine_note: Operating environments change, and uncertainty must be actively monitored.

pattern_cards.description: Assumptions that digital systems, networks, data services, and decision tools remain available and trustworthy.
risk_factors: system access can degrade without visible physical disruption
data services may become stale or unavailable
manual fallbacks may be slower than expected
mitigation_patterns: define minimum data needed for decisions
pattern_cards.safe_historical_analog_summary: Digital availability assumptions can fail when system health and manual alternatives are untested.
pattern_cards.doctrine_note: Commanders need updated information requirements, and assumptions about systems require validation.

pattern_cards.description: Assumptions that phases, handoffs, windows, and sequencing stay synchronized across actors and dependencies.
risk_factors: small delays can cascade across phases
parallel tasks may require precise handoffs
compressed decision windows reduce adaptation time
pattern_cards.safe_historical_analog_summary: Timing assumptions can fail when synchronization depends on unverified readiness.
pattern_cards.doctrine_note: Plans should include branches or sequels, and uncertainty must be actively monitored at decision points.

pattern_cards.description: Assumptions that stress, disruption, or misunderstanding remains contained and does not force broader changes.
risk_factors: local friction can spread across connected tasks
misread signals can increase coordination burden
containment boundaries may be informal or untested
pattern_cards.safe_historical_analog_summary: Plans can become brittle when local friction spreads faster than coordination can adapt.
pattern_cards.doctrine_note: Operating environments change, so commanders need updated information requirements and branch options.

pattern_cards.description: Assumptions that authorities, approvals, permissions, and policy constraints remain aligned with plan timing.
risk_factors: approval timing can block otherwise ready tasks
permissions can change as conditions evolve
authority owner may be unclear
observable_indicators: permission not confirmed before a phase gate
mitigation_patterns: route decisions through validated permission paths
pattern_cards.safe_historical_analog_summary: Permission assumptions can delay plans when authority and timing are not validated.
pattern_cards.doctrine_note: Assumptions require validation, and plans should include branches or sequels for delayed permissions.

pattern_cards.description: Assumptions that personnel, time, equipment, attention, and reserve capacity are sufficient for planned tasks.
risk_factors: resource demand may exceed planning estimates
reserve capacity may be consumed early
critical skills or equipment may not be available when needed
pattern_cards.safe_historical_analog_summary: Resource assumptions can fail when availability, demand, or readiness is not validated.
pattern_cards.doctrine_note: Uncertainty must be actively monitored, and plans should include branch options for resource limits.

pattern_cards.description: Assumptions that command relationships, responsibilities, handoffs, and decision ownership remain clear.
risk_factors: decision ownership may be unclear under friction
handoffs can fail without confirmation
parallel coordination can create conflicting priorities
pattern_cards.safe_historical_analog_summary: Command and coordination assumptions can fail when roles and decision paths are unclear.
pattern_cards.doctrine_note: Commanders need updated information requirements, and assumptions require validation by named owners.

pattern_cards.description: Assumptions that the public information environment, narratives, rumors, and perception remain stable enough for coordination.
risk_factors: rumors can change stakeholder behavior
public messaging may lag operational changes
conflicting narratives can increase coordination demand
mitigation_patterns: reduce scope if perception-driven demand rises
pattern_cards.safe_historical_analog_summary: Information environment assumptions can shift when rumors, messaging, or perception changes.
pattern_cards.doctrine_note: Operating environments change, and uncertainty must be actively monitored through updated information requirements.
